Sea anemones and anemone-like animals on Singapore shores: photo index

Cnidaria > Anthozoa > Actiniaria

Photo index of anemones and anemone-like animals on Singapore shores

a central mouth; a ring of tentacles, or a disk covered with short tentacles (sometimes just bumps). on sand, stuck to hard surfaces (stones, seagrasses, other animals).

Other cnidarians text index | photo index

Diameter < 3cm
including small ones on snail shells

Diameter 3-5cm Long tentacles

Diameter 3-5cm Short tentacles

Diameter 5-10cm

Diameter > 10cm smooth tentacles

Diameter > 10cm frilly tentacles

Cerianthids
also known as peacock or tube anemones

These are NOT sea anemones

This is a hard coral. This is a hard coral.. This is a hard coral. This is a soft coral. This is a soft coral

how to tell apart

  • large ‘hairy’ cnidarians
  • animals with a ring of smooth tentacles
  • animals with a ring of feathery arm

photo index of
cnidarians on this site

**Phylum Cnidaria
with soft body

sea anemones and anemone-like**

Diameter < 3cm

Diameter 3-5cm
  long tentacles
short tentacles Diameter 5-10cm

Diameter >10cm
  smooth tentacles
frilly tentacles

Cerianthids
